title: artemisia cantabrica
intro: Artemisia cantabrica, also known as Cantabric mountain sage, is a perennial herb belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to the Cantabrian Mountains in northern Spain. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its essential oils, particularly thujone and borneol. The health benefits of Artemisia cantabrica include relief from digestive issues, colds, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its essential oil is often used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and ease stress.
